credentials.description: AWS access key ID — must have permissions to create IAM (users, roles, SAML providers), Secrets Manager secrets, and CloudTrail resources
Okta API token with org admin scope — used to provision users, groups, SAML apps, and MFA factors
Okta org domain without https://, e.g. dev-12345678.okta.com
Azure AD tenant ID (GUID) — the directory where the M365 / SharePoint resources are provisioned
Azure AD service principal application (client) ID with Directory.ReadWrite.All and Application.ReadWrite.All
Azure AD service principal client secret
GitHub personal access token with repo and admin:org scopes — used to provision bait repositories containing terraform.tfstate and GitHub Actions secrets
GitHub organization or user name that owns the bait repositories

pulumi_config.description: Okta org domain (without https://) used to configure the AWS SAML identity provider and Okta app assignments
pulumi_config.how_to_get: Found in Okta Admin Console > Settings > Account. Format: dev-XXXXXXXX or your-company.okta.com

pulumi_config.description: Azure AD tenant ID (GUID) — same as ARM_TENANT_ID; stored in Pulumi config for use in SAML app configuration
pulumi_config.how_to_get: Azure Portal > Azure Active Directory > Overview > Tenant ID

pulumi_config.description: GitHub organization or user name that owns the bait repositories
pulumi_config.how_to_get: Your GitHub organization name, e.g. my-company

attack_env_vars.description: Passphrase for Pulumi stack encryption — must match the value used during pulumi up
attack_env_vars.how_to_get: Set any secure passphrase before running pulumi up; reuse the same value here

attack_env_vars.description: Username (email) of the victim Okta user that the phishing attack will target for MFA fatigue
attack_env_vars.how_to_get: The victim user's email address — provisioned by pulumi up; run: pulumi stack output victim_okta_username

attack_env_vars.description: Password for the victim Okta user — set during provisioning
attack_env_vars.how_to_get: Set during infra provisioning; check the Pulumi stack output or the Okta admin console

attack_env_vars.description: Okta API token — attack.py uses it directly to read app assignments and enroll MFA factors on the victim account
attack_env_vars.how_to_get: Okta Admin Console > Security > API > Tokens > Create Token (requires org admin)

attack_env_vars.description: Azure AD application (client) ID used for M365 / SharePoint data access after SAML pivot — resolved from stack outputs if not set
attack_env_vars.how_to_get: Azure Portal > App registrations > your app > Application (client) ID
